picks.thesis: GOOGL tops the technical ranking (13/20) with MACD in bullish expansion and RSI neutral-constructive at 50.6. Cloud revenue at 48% YoY growth and a $240B contracted backlog provide structural upside. The Gemini-Apple deal is a non-consensus catalyst. No earnings within 5 days.

intel.title: Google Cloud 48% YoY
intel.body: $240B backlog, op income +154% YoY. Wiz $32B closed. BMO raised Q1 2026 estimate to 40%.

intel.title: Apple Gemini Partnership
intel.body: Gemini models to power iPhone AI. 750M MAUs. New consumer monetisation channel worth billions annually.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $377 PT
intel.body: 32 Buy / 6 Hold / 0 Sell (TipRanks). +22% upside. Only Mag7 stock with zero sell ratings.

intel.title: Cheapest Mag7 on Fwd P/E
intel.body: 22.9× fwd P/E despite 18% rev growth. Waymo + AI infra are unmodelled optionality.

intel.title: DOJ Antitrust
intel.body: Appeal in progress. Adtech remedies phase. No criminal probe. Structural breakup rejected.

intel.title: CapEx Ramp $140-185B
intel.body: Near-term FCF compression. Consensus treats as growth infra investment, not impairment.

picks.thesis: CRM posts the highest composite conviction score (71). At 13× fwd P/E the market's AI disruption fear has overshot. Agentforce ARR +169% YoY, $72B RPO provides 18 months visibility. $50B buyback signals insider confidence.

intel.title: Agentforce $800M ARR +169%
intel.body: 29,000 deals (+50% QoQ). $72.4B RPO +14% YoY. Q4 FY26 revenue +12% — fastest in 2 years.

intel.title: $50B Buyback Announced
intel.body: "These are low prices" — Benioff. $4.2B returned Q4. Dividend +5.8%. US Army contract active.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $268 PT
intel.body: 28B/7H/1S. +38% upside. BTIG $335: market pricing in "CRM's demise" — too bearish.

intel.title: $72B RPO Backlog
intel.body: 18+ months contracted revenue. 60%+ Agentforce bookings from existing customers.

intel.title: SaaS Model Transition
intel.body: Seat → usage pricing shift creates near-term uncertainty. FY27 guide slightly missed.

intel.title: 13× vs 25× Historical P/E
intel.body: Generational valuation compression. $72B RPO + 77.7% GM + $50B buyback = asymmetric setup.

picks.thesis: MSFT offers the highest-quality fundamental backdrop: Azure 39% YoY, $625B RPO, $74B FCF. Daily RSI 41.1 in recovery zone with MACD bullishly expanding. BB at 0.20 = lower band mean-reversion setup. Beta 0.9 = safest overnight hold.

intel.title: Azure 39% · $625B RPO
intel.body: Intelligent Cloud $32.9B (+29%). $625B commercial RPO +110% YoY including $250B OpenAI commitment.

intel.title: $12.7B Buybacks Q2
intel.body: +32% YoY capital return. FCF $74B. M365 90M+ subscribers. Copilot ARPU expanding.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $495 PT
intel.body: 35B/7H/0S. Foundry $1M+ customers +80% QoQ. OpenAI provider of scale.

intel.title: Copilot ARPU Growth
intel.body: M365 commercial +17% YoY. E5 + Copilot Chat driving ARPU. Dynamics +18%.

intel.title: OpenAI Concentration
intel.body: ~45% of $625B RPO tied to OpenAI. CFO flagged quarterly volatility risk.

intel.title: Beta 0.9 — Safest Hold
intel.body: OS + M365 + Azure + Copilot flywheel. Defensive quality at compressed valuation.

picks.thesis: NVDA ranked #1 technically ahead of GTC 2026 conference. Blackwell GPU demand remains structurally elevated. RSI reset to mid-range after the March correction, creating a clean entry. Target 1 hit on March 6 following GTC announcements.

intel.title: GTC 2026 — Blackwell Demand
intel.body: NVIDIA GTC conference Mar 17-21. Blackwell GPU orders running 12-18 months ahead. H200 still supply-constrained.

intel.title: Data Center Revenue +112%
intel.body: Q4 FY26 data center revenue $30.8B, up 112% YoY. Sovereign AI orders from 40+ governments.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $175 Avg PT
intel.body: 48B/5H/0S. Multiple analysts raised PTs ahead of GTC. +55% upside from entry.

intel.title: China Export Restrictions
intel.body: H20 chip restrictions impacting ~$5B quarterly revenue. Workaround chips face regulatory uncertainty.

intel.title: Valuation Premium
intel.body: Trading at 35× fwd earnings — requires sustained 50%+ revenue growth to justify.

intel.title: Sovereign AI + Robotics
intel.body: Issac robotics platform + digital twin (Omniverse) creating new revenue verticals beyond datacenter.

midweek_update.note: Price held above stop and pushed toward T1 ahead of GTC. RSI recovered to 58. Holding full position.

picks.close_reason: T1 hit at $122.50 (+8.89%). Partial close recommended. Trailing stop set for remainder.

picks.thesis: META entering week with strong AI monetisation tailwinds. Llama 4 launch expected to expand ecosystem. RSI mid-range with MACD bullish. Setup invalidated mid-week on broader tech selloff.

intel.title: AI Ad Revenue +22% YoY
intel.body: AI-driven ad targeting lifting CPMs across all segments. Reels monetisation gap closed with TikTok.

intel.title: Llama 4 Launch Catalyst
intel.body: Open-source Llama 4 release creating enterprise adoption flywheel. 500M+ weekly users on AI features.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $720 PT
intel.body: 42B/4H/0S. +20% upside. Reality Labs losses narrowing as Quest Pro adoption accelerates.

intel.title: EU Regulatory Pressure
intel.body: DMA compliance costs rising. GDPR enforcement actions in Ireland could hit Q2 ad revenue.

intel.title: Macro Selloff Risk
intel.body: High beta (1.4) amplifies downside on broader risk-off moves. Position sizing critical.

intel.title: AR/VR Long-term Play
intel.body: Orion AR glasses prototype demonstrated. 2027-2028 consumer launch could re-rate Reality Labs.

midweek_update.note: Broader tech selloff Thursday. Price tested $578 level. Stop at $572.10 now clearly in range. Recommend reducing position by 50% here.

picks.close_reason: Stopped out at $572.10 (−4.38%). Broader tech selloff overwhelmed the setup. Stop held as designed.

picks.thesis: AMZN showed AWS re-acceleration signals and retail margin expansion. RSI reset to constructive range. BB position 0.29 near lower band. Setup held through the week but did not reach T1.

intel.title: AWS Re-acceleration
intel.body: AWS revenue +22% YoY in Q4. Bedrock AI workloads driving incremental demand. Anthropic partnership deepening.

intel.title: Retail Margin Expansion
intel.body: North America operating margin 7.8% — structural improvement from same-day delivery cost reductions.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $270 PT
intel.body: 46B/5H/0S. AWS still only 18% of total revenue but 70% of operating income.

intel.title: Consumer Macro Sensitivity
intel.body: Discretionary retail segment vulnerable to consumer spending slowdown in H1 2026.

intel.title: Tariff Exposure
intel.body: Chinese goods exposure through third-party sellers could raise platform costs if tariffs escalate.

intel.title: Project Kuiper + Logistics
intel.body: Satellite internet launch 2026-2027. Last-mile logistics becoming standalone profit center.

midweek_update.note: AMZN recovered after early-week dip. Now +2.2% from entry. Did not hit T1 but setup intact. Holding into week close.

picks.close_reason: Closed end of week +2.19%. Did not reach T1. Capital rotated into stronger setups for following week.

picks.thesis: AAPL entered the week at BB lower band with RSI reset. iPhone 17 AI upgrade cycle narrative building. Services revenue reaching new ATH quarter after quarter. T2 hit Friday.

intel.title: iPhone AI Supercycle
intel.body: iPhone 17 expected to drive largest upgrade cycle since iPhone 6. Apple Intelligence features driving upgrades.

intel.title: Services Revenue ATH
intel.body: Services Q1 2026 $26.3B — new all-time high. App Store + AppleTV + Apple Pay compounding.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $290 PT
intel.body: 38B/8H/0S. +29% upside. Berkshire Hathaway remains largest shareholder — Buffett conviction.

intel.title: China Revenue Headwinds
intel.body: Greater China revenue -8% YoY. Huawei competition + geopolitical risk a structural concern.

intel.title: AI Catch-up Risk
intel.body: Apple Intelligence features behind competitors in some categories. Siri overhaul delayed to late 2026.

intel.title: Apple Vision Pro Gen 2
intel.body: Lighter, cheaper Vision Pro Gen 2 expected H2 2026. Spatial computing optionality not in models.

midweek_update.note: T1 hit Wednesday at $238.30 (+6.0%). Strong momentum. Trailing remaining position above entry. T2 at $248.50 in range.

picks.close_reason: T2 hit at $248.50 (+10.54%). Full position closed. Exceptional week.

picks.thesis: AVGO benefiting from hyperscaler custom AI chip demand (Google TPU, Meta MTIA). VMware integration generating strong cross-sell. T1 hit on Thursday.

intel.title: Custom AI Chip Demand
intel.body: Hyperscaler custom silicon (ASIC) revenue +200%+ YoY. Google, Meta, Apple all expanding AVGO partnerships.

intel.title: VMware Integration +$4B Revenue
intel.body: VMware cross-sell generating $4B incremental annual revenue ahead of schedule. Margins expanding.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $235 PT
intel.body: 35B/6H/0S. +24% upside. AI infrastructure spending still in early innings.

intel.title: Huawei/China Exposure
intel.body: Some semiconductor revenue exposed to US-China trade restrictions. Watch Q2 guidance carefully.

intel.title: AI ASIC vs GPU
intel.body: As hyperscalers shift toward custom ASICs, AVGO positioned to take share from NVDA in training workloads by 2028.

midweek_update.note: AVGO +4.8% mid-week, approaching T1. Setup intact. No negative catalysts. On track.

picks.close_reason: T1 hit at $200.20 (+6.26%). Half position closed. Trailing stop set at entry for remainder.

picks.thesis: JPM showing oversold conditions with RSI below 40 and BB position at lower band. Fed pause expectations supportive for NIM. Held through week but did not reach T1.

intel.title: NIM Expansion
intel.body: Net interest margin held above 2.7% as rate environment stabilises. Investment banking revenue recovering.

intel.title: $30B Buyback Programme
intel.body: JPM board approved $30B buyback. CET1 ratio 15.3% — significant excess capital to deploy.

intel.title: Moderate Buy · $310 PT
intel.body: 22B/10H/2S. Dimon cautious on macro but sees value in current valuation.

intel.title: Credit Loss Provisions
intel.body: Consumer credit delinquencies ticking up. Q1 provision build could disappoint.

intel.title: AI in Banking
intel.body: JPM investing $17B in tech in 2026. AI-driven cost efficiencies expected to improve efficiency ratio to sub-55% by 2027.

midweek_update.note: JPM held above stop. Up ~2.5%. Slower mover than tech picks. Holding position.

picks.close_reason: Closed end of week +2.50%. Did not reach T1. Capital rotated into higher-conviction setups.
